kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Exactly one member of the Hill Ward goes with (Potter or Fletcher).
Between Cooper and Potter, one goes with Edmund and the other with Prince.
Exactly two of these are true: Cedric goes with Riverside; Weaver goes with 18 coins; Baron goes with 12 coins.
Between Rowan and Tanner, one goes with Duke and the other with Prince.
Exactly two of these are true: Fletcher goes with Countess; Highrow goes with 14 coins; Kingsford goes with 10 coins.
Potter goes with exactly one of Kingsford, Riverside, or Countess.
Weaver goes with Godfrey if and only if no one in the Joiners' Guild goes with Baron.
Exactly two of the Fletcher, the Weaver, the artisan serving the Duke, and the artisan with 12 coins belong to the River Ward.
The artisan in Riverside's levy is lower than both the Tanner and the artisan serving the Countess.
Either Highrow goes with Godfrey or Potter goes with Duke.
Wulfric goes with exactly two of Weaver, Riverside, Bishop, and 10 coins.
Bishop goes with exactly one of Cooper, Southgate, or 14 coins.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Exactly one member of the East Watershed goes with (Bluegill or Catfish).
Between Bass and Catfish, one goes with Esther and the other with Wobbler.
Exactly two of these are true: Cody goes with Stillwater; Perch goes with 16 lb; Buzzbait goes with 13 lb.
Between Royce and Crappie, one goes with Swimbait and the other with Wobbler.
Exactly two of these are true: Bluegill goes with Spoon; Mossbank goes with 14 lb; Pinehollow goes with 12 lb.
Catfish goes with exactly one of Pinehollow, Stillwater, or Spoon.
Perch goes with Mabel if and only if no one in the Pike Family goes with Buzzbait.
Exactly two of the angler who landed the Bluegill, the angler who landed the Perch, the angler using the Swimbait, and the catch with 13 lb belong to the North Watershed.
The angler at Stillwater's weight is lower than both the angler who landed the Crappie and the angler using the Spoon.
Either Mossbank goes with Mabel or Catfish goes with Swimbait.
Wade goes with exactly two of Perch, Stillwater, Jig, and 12 lb.
Jig goes with exactly one of Bass, Willowmere, or 14 lb.
